things.name: VELTINS-Arena (Schalke Arena)
things.description: Iconic 62,000-capacity arena of Schalke 04 and Shakhtar Donetsk's UEFA venue, one of Germany's most impressive stadiums

things.name: Zoom Erlebniswelt
things.description: Large zoological garden with 900 animals in naturalistic habitats spanning themed zones from Alaska to Africa

things.name: Nordsternpark
things.description: Award-winning landscape park on the former Nordstern colliery site with gardens, climbing structures and art installations

things.name: Musiktheater im Revier
things.description: Renowned opera and ballet theatre opened in 1959, a modernist architectural gem at the heart of Gelsenkirchen's cultural life

things.name: Schloß Horst
things.description: Early Renaissance moated castle ruin of 1578 set in a park, one of the finest Renaissance buildings in Westphalia

things.name: Wissenschaftspark Gelsenkirchen
things.description: Striking post-industrial science and business park in a glass palace designed by Kaup, Scholz and Wilken on a former colliery

things.name: Gelsenkirchen City Centre
things.description: Postwar city centre rebuilt after wartime bombing with Heinrich-König-Platz and the pedestrianised shopping zone

things.name: Consol Theater
things.description: Experimental theatre and cabaret venue occupying the beautifully preserved winding tower of the former Consolidation colliery

things.name: LWL-Industriemuseum Zeche Hannover
things.description: Ruhr industrial heritage museum at the Hannover colliery in nearby Bochum, part of the European Route of Industrial Heritage

things.name: Emscherkunst Outdoor Art Trail
things.description: Open-air contemporary art trail along the Emscher River linking Gelsenkirchen with neighbouring Ruhr cities through post-industrial landscapes
